executiveSummary.text: Base case 55%: risk assets are in a late-cycle AI melt-up pause, not a broken trend. Hot April CPI at 3.8% YoY and oil above $100 pressure discount rates, while S&P 500 slipped 0.16% and Nasdaq 0.71%; confidence: medium-high.

AI semis corrected after a record-driven rally: Qualcomm fell about 11%, Micron about 3.6%, SOXX about 3%. This is valuation/rate sensitivity first, not yet demand-thesis damage for NVDA/AVGO/TSM/ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MU; confidence: medium.

Oil is the dominant macro transmission: WTI around $102 and Brent around $108 keep inflation sticky → Fed cuts delayed → real yields stay high → long-duration growth and REIT multiples face pressure; confidence: medium-high.

Gold around $4,726 is up on the day, but the correct framework is real yields: if oil-led inflation forces higher real rates, gold can underperform cash despite geopolitical stress. Base case 50% range-bound/volatile; confidence: medium.

Singapore remains relatively defensive: STI closed +0.07% at 4,946, DBS +0.56%, OCBC -0.76%. Banks may handle higher-for-longer rates better than REITs, but oil-driven Asia inflation is the second-order risk; confidence: medium.

regime.label: Oil-led inflation shock vs AI earnings momentum

usGlobal.what: US stocks faded from highs after April CPI rose 0.6% MoM / 3.8% YoY and oil stayed above $100; S&P 500 -0.16%, Nasdaq -0.71%, Dow +0.11%.
usGlobal.why: 1st order: CPI and energy lift inflation expectations. 2nd order: fewer Fed cuts and higher real discount rates. 3rd order: long-duration AI leaders can de-rate even if earnings remain strong.
usGlobal.implication: Do not chase strength; prefer pre-set deployment levels. Keep cash barbell valuable while real yields are near 2%.

usGlobal.what: Chip stocks pulled back: Qualcomm dropped about 11%, Micron about 3.6%, and the semiconductor ETF about 3% after a broad AI rally.
usGlobal.why: 1st order: investors took profits in the most rate-sensitive winners. 2nd order: if oil keeps inflation sticky, multiples compress before fundamentals. 3rd order: memory/equipment winners could later benefit if AI capex rotates from GPU scarcity to broader component scarcity.
usGlobal.implication: Treat as watchlist pullback, not thesis break, unless capex guidance or memory pricing weakens.

usGlobal.what: Treasury data show May 12 10Y nominal yield at 4.46% and 10Y real yield at 1.99%; Fed funds remain 3.50%-3.75% per recent Fed/market references.
usGlobal.why: 1st order: higher real yields compete with growth equities and zero-yield gold. 2nd order: cash earns meaningful real carry, raising hurdle rates for risky additions. 3rd order: FI path benefits from patience if volatility creates better entry points.
usGlobal.implication: Cash is not idle; it is a strategic option while real yields stay elevated.

singapore.what: STI closed at 4,946.00, +0.07%; DBS +0.56%, OCBC -0.76% in Yahoo Singapore snapshot.
singapore.why: 1st order: Singapore equities are steadier than US tech during the CPI/oil shock. 2nd order: higher-for-longer rates can support bank net interest margins but pressure REIT valuations. 3rd order: if oil shock hurts Asian growth, credit quality and property demand become the lagged risk.
singapore.implication: Maintain SG dividend sleeve; be selective on REIT top-ups until real-yield pressure eases.

singapore.what: Asia-Pacific markets were mixed as investors weighed renewed US-Iran ceasefire doubts; CNBC Asia update was within the last 24 hours.
singapore.why: 1st order: energy-importing Asian economies face terms-of-trade pressure. 2nd order: inflation limits regional policy easing. 3rd order: SGD and Singapore bank defensiveness may outperform higher-beta Asia, while Korea/semis remain tied to AI cycle volatility.
singapore.implication: Watch Korea/Asia beta if oil remains above $100 and the AI chip rally continues to cool.

commodities.what: OilPrice shows WTI around $102.0 and Brent around $107.8, with headlines in the last 2-18 hours on inventories, Hormuz risk, LNG, and crude-flow disruptions.
commodities.why: 1st order: energy prices lift headline inflation. 2nd order: Fed cannot cut as quickly, keeping real yields high. 3rd order: AI/grid capex costs rise, potentially squeezing hyperscaler margins while supporting energy-infrastructure scarcity assets.
commodities.implication: Oil is the key cross-asset variable today; monitor duration exposure and AI multiple risk.

commodities.what: Gold traded around $4,726 after volatile moves; gold/silver headlines cite pressure from oil-driven inflation and higher-rate concerns.
commodities.why: Gold is a real-rate hedge, not a simple crisis hedge. 1st order: geopolitics can lift uncertainty. 2nd order: if that uncertainty is inflationary, the Fed stays restrictive. 3rd order: higher real yields can offset or overwhelm safe-haven demand.
commodities.implication: Hold strategic metals allocation; avoid adding solely because headlines sound geopolitical.

commodities.what: LNG and shipping headlines show Asia importers and Qatar-related vessel behavior responding to Gulf risks.
commodities.why: 1st order: shipping and LNG rerouting raise energy costs. 2nd order: power costs feed data-center and industrial margins. 3rd order: scarcity thesis benefits energy/grid exposure, but broad equity multiples may compress first.
commodities.implication: AI power bottleneck thesis remains valid, but near-term price action may be dominated by rates.

narratives.title: AI trade broadens, then rate shock tests it
narratives.description: Investors had rotated from Nvidia-only leadership into CPUs, memory, and broader semis; the hot CPI print exposed how much of the move is multiple expansion rather than fresh fundamental news.
narratives.priceImpact: Near-term negative via rates; medium-term positive if AI capex data holds

narratives.title: Oil shock becomes the macro fulcrum
narratives.description: Crude above $100 changes the chain from geopolitical headline to inflation impulse to Fed reaction function. Probability: 45% oil stays elevated 2-4 weeks, 35% de-escalates, 20% worsens into a broader supply shock.
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration assets; mixed-positive for energy infrastructure

narratives.title: Cash regains strategic value
narratives.description: With 10Y real yields near 2%, KL's 26% cash is not a drag; it is optionality against AI-infra volatility and helps preserve the FI target path.
narratives.priceImpact: Supports patience and higher hurdle rates

narratives.title: Singapore defensive carry vs REIT duration pressure
narratives.description: STI stability and bank exposure help the foundation sleeve, but REITs remain sensitive if real yields stay high. Probability: 60% banks outperform REITs short term under higher-for-longer rates.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil > inflation is only first order. The portfolio impact comes through Fed reaction: sticky CPI reduces cut probability, lifts real rates, then compresses QQQ/AI and REIT multiples even if earnings are unchanged.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Use real yields and oil, not headlines, as the action trigger.

secondOrderEffects.text: A semiconductor selloff after a vertical rally may be healthy if AI capex remains intact; but if hyperscalers face higher energy and financing costs, capex discipline could become the third-order risk.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch MSFT/AMZN/GOOG capex commentary and power-cost language, not just chip price action.

secondOrderEffects.text: Gold can fall in a geopolitical inflation shock because the relevant variable is real rates. If inflation pushes the Fed more hawkish and real yields rise, cash competes harder with bullion.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Hold metals as regime insurance; do not add on simplistic safe-haven narratives.

secondOrderEffects.text: Singapore banks may benefit from higher-for-longer rates at first, but if oil inflation damages regional growth, credit quality and loan demand are the lagged third-order risks.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Keep SG dividend exposure but avoid assuming banks are risk-free defensives.

investment.thesis: Keep cash dry for staged AI-infra additions only if the selloff is rate-led, not demand-led.
investment.trigger: Add only on 8-12% pullbacks in high-quality AI infra names or if Nasdaq/semis sell off while capex guidance remains intact.
investment.whyNow: Hot CPI and oil shock create valuation volatility without yet disproving the scarcity thesis.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, memory pricing rollover, or export restrictions that directly impair TSM/ASML/equipment demand.

investment.thesis: Preserve SG dividend foundation; prefer banks over REITs until real yields cool.
investment.trigger: REIT additions only if US 10Y real yield trends below ~1.75% or REIT yields widen enough to compensate duration risk.
investment.whyNow: STI is stable, but real-yield pressure is still hostile to REIT multiples.
investment.invalidator: Singapore credit stress, bank NIM compression, or property/liquidity deterioration.

trading.thesis: Avoid chasing AI semis immediately after record-led rallies; use volatility for disciplined entries.
trading.trigger: Wait for a second down day plus stabilization in yields/oil, or a reclaim of prior support with volume.
trading.whyNow: The current move is driven by CPI/oil/rates rather than confirmed AI demand deterioration.
trading.invalidator: SOXX breaks down alongside negative capex or memory-demand news.

trading.thesis: Oil is the tactical risk switch for QQQ and REIT exposure.
trading.trigger: If Brent sustains above $110 with rising 10Y real yields, reduce short-term risk adds; if Brent falls back below $100 and real yields ease, resume staged buys.
trading.whyNow: Oil is directly feeding the CPI/Fed chain.
trading.invalidator: Equities rally despite higher oil because earnings revisions accelerate enough to offset rates.

watchlist.thesis: Track whether AI leadership rotates into memory/equipment or simply unwinds.
watchlist.trigger: MU, KLAC, LRCX, AMAT stabilizing ahead of NVDA/AVGO would confirm breadth; simultaneous breakdown would signal de-risking.
watchlist.whyNow: CNBC highlighted the shift from Nvidia-only leadership to CPUs/memory before the pullback.
watchlist.invalidator: Memory price hikes fail or orders soften.

watchlist.trigger: Gold holding above recent support while 10Y real yields rise would indicate stronger monetary/geopolitical insurance demand; gold falling with real yields up confirms rate dominance.
watchlist.whyNow: Gold headlines are noisy; the causal driver is real yield behavior.
watchlist.invalidator: Real yields fall materially, shifting gold's setup positive.

riskWatch.name: Oil-led inflation persistence
riskWatch.description: Brent near $108 can keep CPI sticky and reduce Fed flexibility. Transmission: oil → CPI → Fed hold/hawkishness → higher real yields → AI/REIT multiple compression.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not deploy cash aggressively until oil or real yields stabilize; keep barbell intact.

riskWatch.name: AI multiple compression
riskWatch.description: Semis had a strong rally; hot inflation can trigger profit-taking even without thesis damage. The risk becomes fundamental only if capex, memory pricing, or equipment orders weaken.
riskWatch.mitigation: Separate valuation pullback from demand thesis; add only in tranches with invalidators.

riskWatch.name: Gold heuristic error
riskWatch.description: Geopolitical stress can be inflationary rather than disinflationary. If real yields rise, gold can lag cash despite scary headlines.
riskWatch.mitigation: Use 10Y real yield and DXY direction as confirmation before increasing metals.

riskWatch.name: Singapore REIT duration risk
riskWatch.description: Higher real yields pressure REIT multiples and refinancing economics even if distributions look stable.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prioritize balance-sheet quality and wait for yield relief before top-ups.

keyEvents.text: Watch incoming Fed-speaker and FedWatch pricing after hot CPI; market is reassessing how long policy stays at 3.50%-3.75%.
Monitor Brent/WTI and Hormuz/LNG headlines over the next 24-48h; this is the key macro input for rates and AI multiples.
Track US-China meeting headlines on trade and AI; executives including major tech leaders are reportedly involved.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance market live, updated May 13 SGT

keyEvents.text: Watch semiconductor breadth after the pullback: MU, NVDA, AVGO, TSM, ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The decisive variable is not the geopolitical headline itself; it is whether oil keeps inflation high enough to hold real yields near 2%, pressuring AI/growth and REIT multiples.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Stay patient with the 26% cash barbell. Maintain AI scarcity exposure, but add only on rate-led dislocations with unchanged capex fundamentals. Keep SG banks/dividend foundation; be slower on REITs until real yields ease.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore simplistic 'crisis = gold up' and 'chip dip = thesis broken' narratives. Gold depends on real rates; AI semis need capex/order confirmation before changing thesis.
